children.heading: Authorized hazardous fuel reduction projects
children.label: a Authorized hazardous fuel reduction projects

children.text: the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 [ et seq.]; and42 U.S.C. 4321

children.heading: Environmental assessment or environmental impact statement
children.label: b Environmental assessment or environmental impact statement
children.text: The Secretary shall prepare an environmental assessment or an environmental impact statement pursuant to section 102(2) of the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 () for each authorized hazardous fuel reduction project.42 U.S.C. 4332(2)

children.label: B
children.text: the alternative of no action; and

children.label: i
children.text: is proposed during scoping or the collaborative process under subsection (f); and

children.label: ii
children.text: meets the purpose and need of the project, in accordance with regulations promulgated by the Council on Environmental Quality.

children.label: A
children.text: select which additional alternative to consider, which is a choice that is in the sole discretion of the Secretary; and

children.label: B
children.text: provide a written record describing the reasons for the selection.

children.heading: Alternative analysis process for projects in wildland-urban interface
children.label: d Alternative analysis process for projects in wildland-urban interface

children.heading: Proposed agency action and 1 action alternative
children.label: 1 Proposed agency action and 1 action alternative
children.text: For an authorized hazardous fuel reduction project that is proposed to be conducted in the wildland-urban interface, the Secretary is not required to study, develop, or describe more than the proposed agency action and 1 action alternative in the environmental assessment or environmental impact statement prepared pursuant to section 102(2) of the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ().42 U.S.C. 4332(2)

children.heading: Proposed agency action
children.label: 2 Proposed agency action
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (1), but subject to paragraph (3), if an authorized hazardous fuel reduction project proposed to be conducted in the wildland-urban interface is located no further than 1½ miles from the boundary of an at-risk community, the Secretary is not required to study, develop, or describe any alternative to the proposed agency action in the environmental assessment or environmental impact statement prepared pursuant to section 102(2) of the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ().42 U.S.C. 4332(2)

children.heading: Proposed agency action and community wildfire protection plan alternative
children.label: 3 Proposed agency action and community wildfire protection plan alternative
children.text: In the case of an authorized hazardous fuel reduction project described in paragraph (2), if the at-risk community has adopted a community wildfire protection plan and the proposed agency action does not implement the recommendations in the plan regarding the general location and basic method of treatments, the Secretary shall evaluate the recommendations in the plan as an alternative to the proposed agency action in the environmental assessment or environmental impact statement prepared pursuant to section 102(2) of the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ().42 U.S.C. 4332(2)

children.heading: Public notice
children.label: 1 Public notice
children.text: The Secretary shall provide notice of each authorized hazardous fuel reduction project in accordance with applicable regulations and administrative guidelines.

children.label: A
children.text: conduct a public meeting at an appropriate location proximate to the administrative unit of the Federal land on which the authorized hazardous fuel reduction project will be conducted; and

children.label: B
children.text: provide advance notice of the location, date, and time of the meeting.

children.heading: Public collaboration
children.label: f Public collaboration
children.text: In order to encourage meaningful public participation during preparation of authorized hazardous fuel reduction projects, the Secretary shall facilitate collaboration among State and local governments and Indian tribes, and participation of interested persons, during the preparation of each authorized fuel reduction project in a manner consistent with the Implementation Plan.

children.heading: Environmental analysis and public comment
children.label: g Environmental analysis and public comment
children.text: In accordance with section 102(2) of the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 () and the applicable regulations and administrative guidelines, the Secretary shall provide an opportunity for public comment during the preparation of any environmental assessment or environmental impact statement for an authorized hazardous fuel reduction project.42 U.S.C. 4332(2)

children.heading: Decision document
children.label: h Decision document
children.text: The Secretary shall sign a decision document for authorized hazardous fuel reduction projects and provide notice of the final agency actions.
